frameworks.name: geometric morphometrics
frameworks.one_line: shape-space decomposition of facial form into globally vs locally structured components

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: frames whether trait inferences track veridical physiological cues

core_question: Are facial features linked to physiological measurements encoded more globally than facial features driving social-trait attributions?
key_assumptions: facial shape variation can be partitioned into structured (integrated) vs unstructured components
physiological measurements and trait ratings each define a meaningful axis in shape space
manipulations: type of external variable correlated with shape (hormones, BMI, age vs perceived dominance, trustworthiness)
partitioning of covariance matrix into structured vs unstructured parts
key_findings: physiological correlates (hormones, BMI, age) align with globally integrated facial features
attributed traits (dominance, trustworthiness) rely on more locally encoded facial features
interpretations: face-based trait inferences need not be calibrated against true physiology
global vs local encoding dissociates veridical-cue and social-perception channels
